attacking_style: Pure sprinter who does not attack but excels in disorganized, chaotic finales where traditional leadout trains break down. Finds gaps in messy situations within final kilometer.
team_tactics: Team commits domestiques to protect positioning, but lacks elite leadout power to deliver in organized sprints. Relies on rider's individual ability to navigate chaos rather than structured train.
terrain_preference: Reduced bunch sprints and courses with technical finales (roundabouts, tight corners) that fragment leadout organization. Struggles when pure sprint teams control cleanly.
vulnerability: Clean, organized sprints where teams with powerful leadout trains can execute. Lacks the final punch to compete when elite sprint teams control the finale perfectly.
key_patterns: Won both Tour de Pologne stages from chaotic/reduced sprints, 2017 and 2021
Succeeds after long victory droughts when expectations low, peaking unexpectedly as veteran
Exploits disorganized finales: roundabouts destroying leadouts, half-hearted team cooperation in chase
Team protection present but leadout firepower consistently insufficient for clean sprint execution
form_trajectory: positive: 2021 win after long drought suggests ability to peak unexpectedly as veteran, though opportunities remain selective
